message.thinking:
We need to evaluate the given text against three questions:

1. Chapters: Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The text enumerates a list of categories with bullet points, each representing a section. The text lists: "Literatūras un risinājumu analīze", "Izstrādes rīku un bibliotēku novērtējums", "Scenārijs un mijiedarbības dizains", "Simulatora ieviešana", "Eksperimentālā pārbaude un novērtēšana". So yes, it lists main sections. So compliance: true.

But we should mention any missing main sections? The text may not explicitly label them as chapters, but they are listed. So yes, compliance.

2. Description: Does the text briefly describe contents of each section? The text gives description for each bullet: for literature analysis, for tools, for scenario, for simulator, for experimental evaluation. So yes, description is present. compliance: true.

3. Structure: Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? It lists sections and their content; general structure is clear: analysis, evaluation, design, implementation, evaluation. So yes, compliance: true.

But maybe we should comment on missing aspects: maybe not all major parts? Might missing introduction and conclusion? But question only about general structure, from this text. The text gives a clear outline. So compliance true.

Thus produce JSON array with three objects, each with question string: "Chapters", "Description", "Structure".

The feedback should highlight strengths or problems. Since compliance true, mention that text lists main sections and descriptions clearly.

Return JSON array exactly.
